# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!
  before_action :set_report, only: [:show, :execute, :edit, :update, :destroy]

  # Predefined report templates with parameterized queries
  def index
    @reports = Report.where(active: true).order(:name)
  end

  def new
    @report = Report.new
  end

  def create
    @report = Report.new(report_params)
    @report.created_by = current_user

    if @report.save
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), notice: "Report created."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def show
    @parameter_definitions = @report.parameter_definitions
  end

  def edit; end

  def update
    if @report.update(report_params)
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), notice: "Report updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @report.destroy
    redirect_to admin_reports_path, notice: "Report deleted."
  end

  def execute
    @parameter_definitions = @report.parameter_definitions

    unless @report.valid_for_execution?
      flash.now[:alert] = "This report has not been approved for execution."
      render :show, status: :forbidden and return
    end

    sanitized_binds = sanitize_report_params(@report, params[:binds])

    result = ReportExecutor.new(@report, sanitized_binds).execute

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        @columns = result.columns
        @rows = result.rows
        @execution_time = result.execution_time
        render :show
      end
      format.csv do
        send_data result.to_csv, filename: "#{@report.slug}-#{Date.current}.csv"
      end
    end
  rescue ReportExecutor::QueryError => e
    flash.now[:alert] = "Report execution failed: #{e.message}"
    render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
  rescue ReportExecutor::TimeoutError
    flash.now[:alert] = "Report exceeded maximum execution time."
    render :show, status: :request_timeout
  end

  private

  def set_report
    @report = Report.find(params[:id])
  end

  def report_params
    params.require(:report).permit(:name, :description, :category,
                                    :sql_template, :parameter_definitions_json)
  end

  def sanitize_report_params(report, raw_binds)
    return {} if raw_binds.blank?

    report.parameter_definitions.each_with_object({}) do |param_def, hash|
      key = param_def[:name].to_s
      value = raw_binds[key]
      hash[key] = ReportParameterSanitizer.sanitize(value, param_def[:type])
    end
  end

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Access denied."
    end
  end
end


# app/models/report.rb
class Report < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :created_by, class_name: "User"
  belongs_to :approved_by, class_name: "User", optional: true

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :sql_template, presence: true
  validates :slug, presence: true, uniqueness: true

  validate :sql_template_is_safe
  validate :sql_template_uses_only_bind_params

  before_validation :generate_slug, on: :create

  scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }

  FORBIDDEN_KEYWORDS = %w[
    INSERT UPDATE DELETE DROP ALTER CREATE TRUNCATE GRANT REVOKE
    EXEC EXECUTE INTO SET REPLACE MERGE CALL
  ].freeze

  # Parameter definitions stored as JSON
  # Example: [{"name": "start_date", "type": "date", "required": true},
  #           {"name": "department_id", "type": "integer", "required": false}]
  def parameter_definitions
    return [] if parameter_definitions_json.blank?
    JSON.parse(parameter_definitions_json, symbolize_names: true)
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    []
  end

  def valid_for_execution?
    active? && approved_by.present? && approved_at.present?
  end

  def approve!(admin_user)
    update!(approved_by: admin_user, approved_at: Time.current)
  end

  private

  def generate_slug
    self.slug = name&.parameterize
  end

  def sql_template_is_safe
    return if sql_template.blank?

    normalized = sql_template.gsub(/'[^']*'/, "").upcase

    FORBIDDEN_KEYWORDS.each do |keyword|
      if normalized.match?(/\b#{keyword}\b/)
        errors.add(:sql_template, "contains forbidden keyword: #{keyword}")
      end
    end

    # Disallow multiple statements
    statement_count = normalized.scan(/\bSELECT\b/).count
    if normalized.include?(";") || statement_count > 1
      # Allow subqueries but not multiple top-level statements
      if normalized.strip.count(";") > 0
        errors.add(:sql_template, "must not contain multiple statements (no semicolons)")
      end
    end

    # Disallow comments that could hide malicious SQL
    if normalized.match?(/--|\/\*/)
      errors.add(:sql_template, "must not contain SQL comments")
    end
  end

  def sql_template_uses_only_bind_params
    return if sql_template.blank?

    # Ensure the template only uses named bind parameters like :param_name
    # and doesn't contain string interpolation patterns
    if sql_template.match?(/#\{/)
      errors.add(:sql_template, "must not contain Ruby string interpolation")
    end
  end
end


# app/services/report_executor.rb
class ReportExecutor
  class QueryError < StandardError; end
  class TimeoutError < StandardError; end

  QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30
  MAX_ROWS = 10_000

  Result = Struct.new(:columns, :rows, :execution_time, keyword_init: true) do
    def to_csv
      require "csv"
      CSV.generate do |csv|
        csv << columns
        rows.each { |row| csv << row }
      end
    end
  end

  def initialize(report, binds = {})
    @report = report
    @binds = binds
  end

  def execute
    sql = build_safe_query
    start_time = Process.clock_gettime(Process::CLOCK_MONOTONIC)

    result = execute_with_timeout(sql)

    end_time = Process.clock_gettime(Process::CLOCK_MONOTONIC)

    Result.new(
      columns: result.columns,
      rows: result.rows.first(MAX_ROWS),
      execution_time: (end_time - start_time).round(3)
    )
  rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.error("Report execution failed: #{e.message} | Report: #{@report.id}")
    raise QueryError, "Query execution failed. Please check the report definition."
  end

  private

  def build_safe_query
    sql_template = @report.sql_template.dup

    # Use ActiveRecord's sanitization for bind parameters
    @report.parameter_definitions.each do |param_def|
      placeholder = ":#{param_def[:name]}"
      next unless sql_template.include?(placeholder)

      value = @binds[param_def[:name].to_s]

      if value.nil? && param_def[:required]
        raise QueryError, "Required parameter '#{param_def[:name]}' is missing"
      end

      sanitized_value = ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_array(["?", value])
      sql_template.gsub!(placeholder, sanitized_value)
    end

    # Wrap in a read-only, limited query
    wrap_query(sql_template)
  end

  def wrap_query(sql)
    # Add LIMIT to prevent massive result sets
    if sql.match?(/\bLIMIT\b/i)
      sql
    else
      "#{sql} LIMIT #{MAX_ROWS}"
    end
  end

  def execute_with_timeout(sql)
    connection = ActiveRecord::Base.connection

    # Set statement timeout (PostgreSQL)
    if connection.adapter_name.downcase.include?("postgresql")
      connection.execute("SET LOCAL statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'")
    end

    # Use a read-only transaction
    connection.transaction do
      if connection.adapter_name.downcase.include?("postgresql")
        connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY")
      end
      connection.exec_query(sql)
    end
  rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
    if e.message.include?("statement timeout") || e.message.include?("canceling statement")
      raise TimeoutError, "Query exceeded #{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s timeout"
    end
    raise
  end
end


# app/services/report_parameter_sanitizer.rb
class ReportParameterSanitizer
  TYPES = %w[string integer float date datetime boolean].freeze

  def self.sanitize(value, type)
    return nil if value.blank?

    type = type.to_s.downcase
    unless TYPES.include?(type)
      raise ArgumentError, "Unknown parameter type: #{type}"
    end

    send(:"sanitize_#{type}", value)
  end

  def self.sanitize_string(value)
    # Limit length and strip dangerous characters
    value.to_s.strip.first(500)
  end

  def self.sanitize_integer(value)
    Integer(value)
  rescue ArgumentError, TypeError
    raise ReportExecutor::QueryError, "Invalid integer value: #{value}"
  end

  def self.sanitize_float(value)
    Float(value)
  rescue ArgumentError, TypeError
    raise ReportExecutor::QueryError, "Invalid float value: #{value}"
  end

  def self.sanitize_date(value)
    Date.parse(value.to_s)
  rescue Date::Error
    raise ReportExecutor::QueryError, "Invalid date value: #{value}"
  end

  def self.sanitize_datetime(value)
    Time.zone.parse(value.to_s) || raise(ArgumentError)
  rescue ArgumentError
    raise ReportExecutor::QueryError, "Invalid datetime value: #{value}"
  end

  def self.sanitize_boolean(value)
    ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(value)
  end

  private_class_method :sanitize_string, :sanitize_integer, :sanitize_float,
                       :sanitize_date, :sanitize_datetime, :sanitize_boolean
end


# db/migrate/XXXXXXXXXX_create_reports.rb
class CreateReports < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    create_table :reports do |t|
      t.string :name, null: false
      t.string :slug, null: false
      t.text :description
      t.string :category
      t.text :sql_template, null: false
      t.text :parameter_definitions_json
      t.boolean :active, default: false, null: false
      t.references :created_by, null: false, foreign_key: { to_table: :users }
      t.references :approved_by, foreign_key: { to_table: :users }
      t.datetime :approved_at

      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :reports, :slug, unique: true
    add_index :reports, :name, unique: true
    add_index :reports, :active
  end
end
